terms.name: Where to get character cards?
terms.desc: [[character]] cards in SillyTavern format are available on several community websites. [Chub.ai](https://chub.ai) (Character Hub) is the largest repository — search, preview, and download cards directly as .json files ready to import into Glaze. For characters from Janitor AI, use [datacat.run](https://datacat.run) to export any character as a card file — including private ones and those with proxy disabled. [Backyard.ai](https://backyard.ai) also has a library of cards you can download.

terms.name: Why is the model thinking inside the message?
terms.desc: The model is sending its reasoning (chain of thought) inside the response text rather than in a separate block. See [[preset-reasoning|Reasoning]] — enable 'Parse Inline Reasoning' to automatically extract thinking from tags like <think>...</think> and display it in a separate reasoning block.

terms.name: Where to find an LLM?
terms.desc: You need an [[api|API provider]] — a service that gives you access to AI models. After signing up, you'll get an API endpoint and key to enter in Glaze's API settings.

terms.name: The model is censoring my messages
terms.desc: Try a good preset — for example, fawnie from the built-in presets. Make sure to use at least inline CoT (the additional thinking feature in the preset). Note that censorship can also depend on the provider, even if the model is the same. It's not just the chat that matters — all data in the preset affects this, including your [[persona]] and [[character]] card. The model can be triggered by sexual descriptions in them.

terms.name: Which models should I use?
terms.desc: A few options for different tastes and budgets. Bang for the buck — DeepSeek V3.2. Middle ground — Gemini 3.1 Pro. Flagship model — Claude Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6. For those tired of the rest — GLM 5.1. Others aren't really worth the attention — either because of poor context size or mediocre writing.

terms.name: LLM
terms.desc: An AI that reads and writes text. It works by looking at your entire conversation and predicting what word comes next, one piece at a time. Examples: Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Deepseek V3.2.

terms.name: Token
terms.desc: The smallest piece of text the AI reads and writes — roughly 3/4 of a word. Every message you type and every reply you get is counted in tokens. There's also a limit: once the conversation is too long, the oldest messages get cut off. More tokens = higher cost and slower replies.

terms.name: Context
terms.desc: The maximum amount of [[token]]s the AI can see at once — its working memory. Everything Glaze sends (system instructions, [[preset]] blocks, [[lorebook]] entries, chat history, and the new reply) must fit inside this window. When the conversation grows too long, older messages are trimmed to stay within the limit. Context Size is set in model settings and must not exceed the model's maximum.

terms.name: API
terms.desc: Typically a service that provides a special link — an endpoint — and a key to access it. Glaze connects to this endpoint to send your messages to the AI and receive replies. Must support the OpenAI-compatible format. Recommended providers: [OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai) and [LinkAPI](https://linkapi.ai).

terms.name: API Key
terms.desc: A secret code that proves you have permission to use an AI service. Never share it — anyone who has it can use the service as if they were you.

terms.name: Streaming
terms.desc: When turned on, the AI sends its reply word by word as it writes — like watching someone type in real time. When turned off, you wait for the full reply before seeing anything. May provoke stronger censorship from the model. Recommended to disable.

terms.name: Chat Completion
terms.desc: The message format Glaze uses to talk to the AI: a list of messages, each labeled as 'system' (hidden instructions), 'user' (your messages), or 'assistant' (AI replies). Glaze assembles this list automatically from your [[preset]] blocks.

terms.name: Character
terms.desc: An AI character card — a description of who the AI is playing. Includes the character's name, personality, backstory, scenario, example dialogues, and avatar. A character can have multiple independent [[chat-session]]s, its own [[lorebook]] entries, and custom settings via [[connections]].

terms.name: Persona
terms.desc: Your in-app identity for the roleplay. A persona has a name, an avatar, and a prompt that describes who 'you' are in the story. The prompt is included in the AI's context. You can have multiple personas and assign different ones to different characters or chats.

terms.name: Chat Session
terms.desc: A separate conversation branch with a character. Each session has its own full message history, [[authornote]], and [[summary]]. You can create as many sessions as you like with the same character — for example, to explore different story paths without overwriting your existing chat.

terms.name: Connections
terms.desc: Links a [[lorebook]], [[preset]], or [[persona]] to a specific scope. Scopes: globally (all chats), per character, or per individual chat session. Priority: chat > character > global. Lets different characters or conversations automatically use different settings without manual switching.

terms.name: Swipes
terms.desc: Multiple generated replies for the same message position. If you don't like the AI's reply, swipe or tap arrows to see another version. Only the version you land on is included in the conversation. You can also request a [[guided-swipe]] to steer the next attempt in a specific direction.

terms.name: Guided Generation
terms.desc: A [[swipes]] variant where you add a one-time instruction before regenerating. For example: 'make the scene more tense' or 'have the character mention the forest'. The instruction steers just this one generation. It is saved in the chat history for convenience, but is not sent to the AI in subsequent requests.

terms.name: Impersonation
terms.desc: Generate a reply as if you were your own character — useful for writing your side of the dialogue when you're not sure what to say. Uses a [[guided]] instruction block to tell the AI to write from your perspective instead of the character's.

terms.name: Selection Mode
terms.desc: Lets you select multiple messages at once for bulk deletion or hiding. Activate by long-pressing a message, then tap checkboxes on each message you want to include.

terms.name: Hidden Message
terms.desc: A message that is excluded from what gets sent to the AI but still visible in your chat. Useful for pruning old or unwanted messages to save space without permanently deleting them. Hidden messages appear faded with a small indicator. To hide a message, select it using [[selection-mode]].

terms.name: Chat Session
terms.desc: A separate conversation branch with a character. Each session has its own full message history, [[authornote]], and [[summary]]. You can create as many sessions as you like with the same character — for example, to explore different story paths without overwriting your existing chat.

terms.name: Connections
terms.desc: Links a [[lorebook]], [[preset]], or [[persona]] to a specific scope. Scopes: globally (all chats), per character, or per individual chat session. Priority: chat > character > global. Lets different characters or conversations automatically use different settings without manual switching.

terms.name: Quick Access
terms.desc: A side panel that slides in during a chat. All necessary chat settings are located here: [[character]] info, active [[lorebook]] entries, Author's Note, summary, sessions, preset, and persona settings. Open it with the wand button in the chat input area.

terms.name: Triggered Items
terms.desc: An icon [icon:triggered-items] that appears on a message when at least one [[lorebook]] entry or [[regex]] script was activated during its generation. Tap it to see exactly which lorebook entries were injected and which regex scripts were applied to that message.

terms.name: Request Preview
terms.desc: A very handy tool to debug your prompt. It shows the exact [[chatcompletion|JSON Chat Completion]] payload Glaze is sending to the [[api|API provider]]. It's useful for debugging prompt issues, seeing how [[macro]]s are expanded, and checking which [[lorebook]] entries and [[regex]] scripts are being injected. You can view the request both in a formatted view and as raw JSON.

terms.name: Image Generation
terms.desc: A background service that monitors messages for image generation tags (like `[IMG:GEN]`). When a tag is detected, it handles the API request to OpenAI, Google, or Naistera to generate an inline image directly in the chat. It can also send visual references — such as character avatars or your own custom images — to help the AI maintain consistency in the generated art.

terms.name: Preset
terms.desc: A customizable set of instructions Glaze sends to the AI before every reply. Made up of blocks you can turn on/off and reorder: System (global rules), Persona (who you are), [[character]] card, Scenario, Example dialogues, [[summary]], [[authornote]], and Chat History. Assignable globally, per character, or per [[chat-session]] via [[connections]].

terms.name: Macro
terms.desc: A placeholder in {{curly braces}} that gets automatically replaced with real content when Glaze builds the [[preset]] prompt. Common examples: {{char}} (character name), {{user}} (your name), {{description}}, {{scenario}}, {{random::a::b}} (random choice), {{roll::1d20}} (dice roll). Also works in [[regex]] replacement strings.

terms.name: Author's Note
terms.desc: A short note you can write separately for each [[chat-session]]. It's placed near the very end of the conversation in the AI's view, giving it strong influence over the next reply. Great for current plot reminders, style directions, or anything the AI should keep in mind right now.

terms.name: Summary
terms.desc: A block for a brief recap of the earlier parts of the chat. As conversations grow long, the oldest messages fall out of the AI's memory — a summary helps it stay aware of what happened earlier. You can write it yourself or generate it automatically with a button.

terms.name: Guided Generation
terms.desc: Sends a one-time instruction alongside the next message to steer the AI's reply — for example, directing the plot, tone, or writing style. Also refers to the sampling settings that shape how the AI picks words: [[temperature]], [[top-p]], and similar parameters.

terms.name: Temperature
terms.desc: Controls how creative or predictable the AI's replies are. Low (0.1-0.5) = safe and focused, but can become repetitive. High (1.0-2.0) = varied and creative, but may turn incoherent. For roleplay, 0.7-1.2 is a good starting range.

terms.name: Top-P
terms.desc: Limits the pool of words the AI can pick from at each step. At 1.0, everything is fair game. At 0.9, it only considers the most likely 90% of words, cutting out rare and unusual choices. Lower values = more focused and predictable replies.

terms.name: Message Role
terms.desc: Each message in the conversation has a label that affects how the AI perceives it. 'system' — an instruction from the system, the AI treats it almost as a given. 'user' — triggers the model's user-interaction behavior patterns. 'assistant' — the model thinks it wrote this itself (prefill), and tends to repeat the style.

terms.name: Injection Point
terms.desc: Controls where a block appears in the list of messages sent to the AI. 'Relative' keeps it in its natural order among the blocks. 'Depth' inserts it a fixed number of messages before the end — for example, depth 2 places it just before the last 2 chat messages, giving it strong immediate influence.

terms.name: Merge Prompts
terms.desc: Combines neighboring blocks that share the same role label into one message. Reduces the total message count. Some AI models respond better to fewer, longer messages rather than many short ones. Required for NoAssistant mode.

terms.name: NoAssistant
terms.desc: A special mode that puts the entire conversation into one big block of text with 'User:' and 'Character:' labels, instead of sending separate messages. Helps reduce the model's predisposition toward the user. Requires a character card in a special format and third-person messages — the user must be hidden from the model.

terms.name: Stop String
terms.desc: A word or phrase that tells the AI to stop generating immediately when it writes that text. In NoAssistant mode, this prevents the AI from writing your character's lines — for example, adding 'User:' as a stop string makes the AI stop before it tries to reply on your behalf.

terms.name: Reasoning
terms.desc: Some AI models 'think out loud' before giving their final answer, working through their reasoning step by step. 'Request Model Reasoning' only affects visibility and does not disable the model's thinking process. 'Parse Inline Reasoning' is made for cases when the model sends its thinking inside the response text rather than in a separate block — it extracts it from tags like <think>...</think> and displays it separately.

terms.name: Parse Inline Reasoning
terms.desc: Automatically extracts thinking/reasoning from the model's main message body and moves it to a separate UI block. This is especially useful for models like DeepSeek V3 that use tags like `<think>...</think>` instead of a dedicated reasoning field, and for presets designed to bypass native reasoning features by forcing the model to 'think out loud' directly in its response text.

terms.name: Lorebook
terms.desc: A collection of information entries automatically added to the AI's memory when certain keywords appear in the chat. Think of it like a secret encyclopedia — when a place name, character name, or topic comes up, the relevant entry is silently included. Great for world details, [[character]] backstories, and lore. Control what triggers entries with [[lorebook-keys]] and how much space they use with [[lorebook-budget]].

terms.name: Primary / Secondary Keys
terms.desc: The keywords that trigger a lorebook entry. When a primary keyword appears in the recent chat, the entry activates. Secondary keys act as an extra filter: the entry only fires if those are present too. The exact combination logic — AND, OR, NOT — is set in [[lorebook-logic]]. To see which entries actually fired for a given message, check [[triggered-items]].

terms.name: Logic Mode
terms.desc: Controls how primary and secondary keywords combine to decide if an entry activates. 'Primary Only' — secondary keywords are ignored. 'AND Any/All' — secondary keywords must also be present. 'NOT Any/All' — the entry only fires if secondary keywords are absent.

terms.name: Context Percent / Budget Cap
terms.desc: Limits how much of the AI's memory lorebook entries can occupy. The AI can only hold so much text at once — this controls what share lorebooks are allowed to use. Context Percent sets a relative cap (e.g. 20%). Budget Cap sets a hard token limit. Entries are sorted by priority and added until the budget runs out.

terms.name: Probability
terms.desc: Even when an entry's keywords match, it only activates with this probability (0-100%). Use it to add randomness — for example, a 50% chance means a character's backstory is only included half the time their name appears.

terms.name: Sticky / Cooldown / Delay
terms.desc: Three timing controls for entries. Sticky: the entry stays active for N turns after its keywords stop appearing. Cooldown: after triggering, the entry won't fire again for N turns. Delay: the entry won't activate at all until N turns have passed in the chat.

terms.name: Group Scoring
terms.desc: Entries in the same named group compete: only the one with the highest weight activates, not all of them at once. Useful for mutually exclusive facts — for example, different weather descriptions for the same location.

terms.name: Recursive Scan
terms.desc: After adding lorebook entries to the AI's context, Glaze re-checks the newly added text for more keyword matches. This creates chains — one entry can trigger another, like a linked encyclopedia. Use with caution: it can cause loops where entries keep triggering each other.

terms.name: Regex Scripts
terms.desc: Special scripts that find text matching a specified pattern and replace it with a specified template. Control what kind of text it affects via [[regex-placement]]. To see which scripts were applied to a specific message, check [[triggered-items]].

terms.name: Placement
terms.desc: Controls which text the script runs on: 'User Input' (your messages before sending), 'AI Output' (the model's replies), 'World Info' (lorebook content), or 'Reasoning' (model thinking). A single script can target multiple text types at once.

terms.name: Ephemerality
terms.desc: 'Alter Chat Display' — only what you see on screen changes; the stored text and what the AI receives stay the same. 'Alter Outgoing Prompt' — the AI actually receives the modified version. Both options can be active at the same time.

terms.name: Trim Out
terms.desc: A list of strings (one per line) that are stripped from all regex matches before the replacement is applied. Useful for cleaning up unwanted characters that should always be removed, regardless of what triggered the match.

terms.name: Macros in Find Regex
terms.desc: Whether [[macro]] placeholders like {{char}} and {{user}} are expanded in the search pattern before matching. 'Don't substitute' — match the literal text {{char}}. 'Substitute raw' — replace with the actual character name first. 'Substitute escaped' — replace and escape special regex characters so they match literally.

terms.name: Themes
terms.desc: Customize how the app looks. Switch between light and dark mode, change the accent color, set custom colors for message bubbles, pick fonts for the UI and chat, and control the transparency and blur of background elements. Theme settings apply globally across all chats.
